title: SILAC DMEM 1 recipe
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bc1jb","text":"Media recipe for our standard DMEM for SILAC media.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3fo06","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d5rkk","text":"Notes:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"61ihl","text":"Van originally used 50mg/500ml for all RKP but there was a very small amount of R10-\u003eP6 conversion visible in MS1 for abundant peptides","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6nc5o","text":"Dialysed FBS: Label with dialysed FBS batch so that L and H media with the same dFBS can be used in experiment. Freeze thaw cycles should be limited, so thaw 500ml bottle and make 50ml aliquots for adding to cell culture media and store at -20°C. Thaw at 4°C or at 37°C, ensuring to mix throughout thawing to prevent salt/protein gradients which increase precipitates","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dh4dj","text":"Amino acids: Make up arginine, lysine and proline to 0.1g/ml in UltraPure water (Invitrogen; 10977035), filter-sterilise using 0.2um filters and aliquot","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 500ml DMEM for SILAC (Thermo A33822) Deficient in arginine and lysine
50ml dialysed FBS (Gibco, 26400044) Spin 3202g 3 min before adding to remove precipitate

Arginine (Heavy Arginine.HCl: Sigma, 608033 or Light Arginine: Sigma, A8094-100G) 250ul heavy from 0.1g/ml stock (25mg/500ml; 50mg/L; 227uM) 197.4ul light from 0.1g/ml stock (19.74mg/500ml; 39.485mg/L; 227uM)

Lysine (Heavy Lysine.HCl; Sigma 608041 or Light Lysine.HCl Sigma, L8662-100G) 500ul heavy from 0.1g/ml stock (50mg/500ml; 100mg/L; 525uM) 479.2ul light from 0.1g/ml stock (47.94mg/500ml; 95.834mg/L; 525uM)
creator: Victoria Munro
